info.title: uapi
info.description:
Native HTTP REST API for OpenWrt. Translates standard REST verbs into ubus/uci operations so edge routers become first-class targets for Infrastructure-as-Code workflows.

## Quickstart

Mint a token on the router (one-time):

```sh
uapi-token create --name terraform_prod --scope '*:rw' --expires-in 90d
```

Then call the API:

```sh
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" https://router/api/v2/firewall/rules
```

## Two surfaces

- **Curated resources** under `/api/v2/<domain>/...` - hand-written schemas, stable across the major. Field names are `snake_case`; uci booleans normalize to JSON booleans; uci list options surface as JSON arrays.
- **Raw passthrough** under `/api/v2/raw/<package>/<id>` - generic uci access for the long tail. Same atomic-transaction recipe and same auth model, but payloads follow uci's field names directly (and move when upstream OpenWrt does).

## Resource shape

Every curated resource carries `id` (stable across uci rewrites) and `managed: bool` at the top level. Server-derived state lives under `runtime: {...}` (computed; clients ignore for drift detection).

## Auth

Bearer tokens with hierarchical scopes (e.g. `firewall:rules:rw`, `*:ro`). See the **Auth / Tokens** group for mint/list/revoke and the `/auth/whoami` endpoint for introspection.

## Optimistic concurrency

Every resource GET and write returns an `ETag` header that is a stable hash of the resource's own body (the `runtime` block is excluded so live ubus state never trips a 412). Honor with `If-Match` on writes (or `?if_match=<etag>` query param for clients behind uhttpd's strict CGI env, which drops the header). Conditional GET via `If-None-Match` returns 304 when matching. Sibling sections in the same package do not influence each other's ETags; If-Match fires only when *this* resource has actually changed.

## Idempotency

`Idempotency-Key` on POST caches the response for 24 h; a repeat with the same key replays. Same key with a different body returns `409 idempotency_key_conflict`.

## Sensitive fields (write-only + `has_<field>` presence flag)

Fields holding secret material (passphrases, private keys, PSKs, PKCS#12 paths) are write-only on the wire: GET responses omit the value and surface a read-only `has_<field>: bool` companion indicating presence. Examples: `wireless.interfaces.key`/`has_key`, `network.wireguard_peers.private_key`/`has_private_key`, `network.wireguard_peers.preshared_key`/`has_preshared_key`, `openvpn.instances.key`/`has_key`, `openvpn.instances.tls_auth`/`has_tls_auth`, `openvpn.instances.pkcs12`/`has_pkcs12`. PATCH that omits a sensitive field carries the existing value forward; rotation is explicit.

## Atomicity

Every write is one transaction: snapshot, validate, commit, reload, restore-on-failure. `POST /batch` extends this across N packages under one combined snapshot/restore.

## IMPORTANT - Success != runtime convergence

A 2xx response means the init script's reload action **exited 0**. It does NOT mean the daemon has finished re-converging (`network/interfaces` is the dangerous one: a bad change can drop the management link, and the API has already reported success). The `X-Reload-Status` response header surfaces the reload outcome explicitly:

- `X-Reload-Status: ok` - init script ran and exited 0 (not a convergence promise)
- `X-Reload-Status: no_reload` - the resource has no reload services

For high-stakes writes (management interface, firewall defaults, uhttpd itself) verify convergence out-of-band. See [`docs/operations.md`](https://github.com/raspbeguy/uapi/blob/main/docs/operations.md) `Success != converged` for the full contract.

## Compatibility & versioning

A given uapi installation serves exactly one API major. Within a major, additions are backwards-compatible: new endpoints, new optional fields, new error codes, new scopes. Breaking changes require the next major. Operators who need an older major keep that package version installed.

## More

- **GitHub:** https://github.com/raspbeguy/uapi
- **Terraform provider:** https://registry.terraform.io/providers/raspbeguy/uapi
- **APK feed install:** [/install/](../install/)
- **Architecture, security, migration, release-process docs:** [in repo](https://github.com/raspbeguy/uapi/tree/main/docs)

tags.name: Firewall / Zones
tags.description: Firewall zones (`config zone`): input/output/forward policies, network lists, masq/mtu_fix toggles.

tags.name: Firewall / Rules
tags.description: Firewall rules (`config rule`): nested `match` block for src/dest zone, IPs, ports, proto, family. Cross-refs `firewall/zones`.

tags.name: Firewall / Redirects
tags.description: Port forwards and NAT loopback (`config redirect`). Cross-refs `firewall/zones`.

tags.name: Firewall / Forwardings
tags.description: Zone-to-zone forwarding (`config forwarding`).

tags.name: Firewall / Defaults
tags.description: Global firewall defaults singleton: verdicts, syn_flood, tcp_syncookies, flow_offloading.

tags.name: Network / Interfaces
tags.description: Network interfaces (`config interface`). proto static/dhcp/dhcpv6/pppoe/wireguard/etc. `runtime` carries ubus state.

tags.name: Network / Devices
tags.description: Network devices (`config device`): bridges, VLAN (8021q/8021ad), macvlan, veth, tun/tap.

tags.name: Network / Routes
tags.description: Static routes (`config route`).

tags.name: Network / Rules
tags.description: Policy routing rules (`config rule`).

tags.name: Network / Bridge Vlans
tags.description: Bridge VLAN tagging (`config bridge-vlan`).

tags.name: Network / Wireguard Peers
tags.description: WireGuard peers (dynamic uci type `wireguard_<iface>`); preshared_key write-only.

tags.name: Wireless / Devices
tags.description: Wifi radios (`config wifi-device`): band, channel, htmode, country, txpower.

tags.name: Wireless / Interfaces
tags.description: SSIDs (`config wifi-iface`). `key` write-only; runtime carries iwinfo state.

tags.name: Dhcp / Hosts
tags.description: Static DHCP leases (`config host`).

tags.name: Dhcp / Servers
tags.description: Per-interface DHCP server config (`config dhcp`). runtime carries lease counts.

tags.name: Dhcp / Dnsmasq
tags.description: Global dnsmasq tuning singleton.

tags.name: Dhcp / Odhcpd
tags.description: odhcpd singleton.

tags.name: Dhcp / Leases
tags.description: IPv4 leases parsed from /tmp/dhcp.leases (read-only).

tags.name: Dhcp / Leases6
tags.description: IPv6 leases from odhcpd statefile (read-only).

tags.name: System
tags.description: Global system config singleton (`config system`): hostname, timezone, log_size, etc.

tags.name: System / Timeservers
tags.description: NTP server list (`config timeserver`).

tags.name: System / Password
tags.description: Local Unix password (write-only; shells out to passwd).

tags.name: System / SSH authorized keys
tags.description: Manage `/etc/dropbear/authorized_keys` entries by stable id.

tags.name: Dropbear / Instances
tags.description: Per-instance SSH config (`config dropbear`).

tags.name: Uhttpd / Instances
tags.description: Per-instance HTTP server config (`config uhttpd`). Self-lockout protection on `main`.

tags.name: Uhttpd / Certs
tags.description: px5g certificate generation params (`config cert`).

tags.name: Unbound / Server
tags.description: Recursive DNS server singleton (`config unbound`).

tags.name: Sqm / Queues
tags.description: Per-interface SQM shaping (`config queue`).

tags.name: Snmpd / Agents
tags.description: SNMP listen addresses (`config agent`).

tags.name: Snmpd / Com2secs
tags.description: community-to-security mapping (`config com2sec`).

tags.name: Snmpd / Groups
tags.description: SNMP groups (`config group`).

tags.name: Snmpd / Accesses
tags.description: group-to-view ACLs (`config access`).

tags.name: Snmpd / System
tags.description: SNMPv2-MIB system.* singleton (sys_location, sys_contact, etc.).

tags.name: Lldpd / Config
tags.description: LLDP/CDP/etc. toggles singleton.

tags.name: Prometheus Node Exporter Lua / Config
tags.description: node_exporter listen + per-collector toggles singleton.

tags.name: Vnstat / Config
tags.description: Global vnstat singleton.

tags.name: Vnstat / Interfaces
tags.description: Per-iface vnstat enable.

tags.name: Mwan3 / Globals
tags.description: mwan3 multi-WAN tuning singleton (mark mask, logging, route-monitor interval).

tags.name: Mwan3 / Interfaces
tags.description: Per-WAN tracking config: track_ip probes, failure/recovery thresholds, family.

tags.name: Mwan3 / Members
tags.description: (interface, metric, weight) tuples consumed by policies.

tags.name: Mwan3 / Policies
tags.description: Member groups with a last-resort fallback. Cross-refs `mwan3:members`.

tags.name: Mwan3 / Rules
tags.description: Traffic-match -> policy bindings. Cross-refs `mwan3:policies`.

tags.name: Usteer / Config
tags.description: Passive band-steering daemon singleton (signal thresholds, roam scan intervals, SSID filter).

tags.name: Openvpn / Instances
tags.description: Per-tunnel OpenVPN config. Filesystem paths for ca/cert/dh; key/tls_auth/pkcs12 are write-only (reads return has_<field>).

tags.name: Raw / Generic uci passthrough
tags.description: Escape hatch for any uci section type uapi does not curate. Same atomic-transaction recipe, same auth model. Stable URL/verb/error contract; payload follows uci's moving target.

tags.name: Packages / Installed
tags.description: Manage on-router apk packages (shells out to `apk add`/`del`).

tags.name: Packages / Feeds
tags.description: Manage `/etc/apk/repositories.d/*.list` feed files.

tags.name: Auth / Whoami
tags.description: Token introspection: read the calling bearer's own metadata.

tags.name: Auth / Tokens
tags.description: HTTP token rotation: list, mint, revoke. Mint enforces scope-subset (caller must hold every requested scope).

tags.name: Operational / Healthz
tags.description: Liveness + subsystem checks. No auth. Treat `version` as the stable version-skew probe.

tags.name: Operational / OpenAPI spec
tags.description: Self-describing endpoint serving this OpenAPI document. No auth.

tags.name: Operational / Schema discovery
tags.description: Per-resource schema_properties for dynamic clients without parsing the full spec. No auth.

tags.name: Operational / Metrics
tags.description: Prometheus 0.0.4 text. Path-template labels normalize concrete ids.

tags.name: Operational / Diagnostics
tags.description: Lock state, uptime, loaded resources.

tags.name: Operational / Batch
tags.description: Multi-package atomic transaction (max 50 ops). 207 Multi-Status on success.

parameters.name: managed
parameters.description: Filter by managed flag

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Section identifier (uapi-generated ULID for managed sections, uci cfg-name for unmanaged)

get.description: Supports conditional GET via `If-None-Match` (or `?if_none_match=` query param for clients behind uhttpd's strict CGI env). A matching ETag returns 304 with no body.

patch.description: Default content-type uses RFC 7396 merge-patch semantics (partial object). `application/json-patch+json` selects RFC 6902 JSON Patch with ops add/remove/replace/move/copy/test (the test op enables atomic compare-and-swap without If-Match).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Section identifier (uapi-generated ULID for managed sections, uci cfg-name for unmanaged)

post.description: Adopt an anonymous firewall rule into uapi management. The section is renamed to a uapi-generated ULID and `managed` flips to `true`; subsequent writes (PUT, PATCH, DELETE) are then permitted. **The id changes**: the response carries the new ULID; any client state pointing at the original anonymous id (`cfgXXXXXX`) must be updated. Idempotent: adopting an already-managed section returns 409 unmanaged_resource (see error envelope).

Add a single SSH public key

delete.summary: Remove a single SSH key by stable id

get.summary: Retrieve this OpenAPI document (no auth required)
List every curated resource key (no auth)
Schemas for every resource in one package (no auth)
Schema for one curated resource (no auth)
Introspection: the calling token's own metadata
get.description: No additional scope check. Any authenticated bearer can read its own metadata.
Scope: uapi:tokens:ro (or *:ro). Each entry omits salt and hash.

post.summary: Mint a new token over HTTP
post.description: Scope: uapi:tokens:rw (or *:rw). Requested scopes MUST be a strict subset of the caller's own; escalation returns 403 scope_escalation_blocked. The cleartext bearer is returned exactly once.

get.description: Scope: uapi:metrics:ro (or *:ro). Series: uapi_requests_total, uapi_request_duration_seconds_bucket, uapi_request_duration_seconds_count, uapi_rate_limit_drops_total, uapi_lock_contention_total, uapi_validate_errors_total. Path-template labels normalize concrete ids to :id to keep cardinality bounded.

get.summary: Operational snapshot (lock state, uptime, loaded resources)
get.description: Scope: uapi:diagnostics:ro (or *:ro).

post.description: Each sub-request is scope-checked independently. Pure-read batches acquire no lock. Writes acquire per-package EX locks in sorted order (deadlock-free) under one combined snapshot/restore. First sub-request failure aborts the batch and reverts all packages; success returns 207 Multi-Status with the per-sub-request results. Max 50 ops.

code.description: Machine-readable error code. Stable within a major. Clients should branch on HTTP status first and treat unknown codes gracefully (the project's additive contract permits new codes within a major).

request_id.description: ULID echoed in the X-Request-Id response header. Pair with audit log for server-side tracing.

aborted_at_index.description: batch_partial_failure only: 0-based index of the sub-request that failed.

reverted.description: batch_partial_failure only: true if all packages were restored.

error.description: batch_partial_failure only: the failing sub-request's envelope.

id.description: Optional; generated if absent
Package name (same as name)

url.description: HTTP(S) URL of the package repository

user.description: Local Unix user to update; usually 'root'

password.description: New password (min 8 chars). Never echoed back.

id.description: Stable id: sha256 prefix of the public-key blob (or non-crypto djb2 fallback in test environments)

type.description: SSH key type (e.g. ssh-ed25519, ssh-rsa, ecdsa-sha2-nistp256)

comment.description: Optional comment (trailing text on the key line)

version.description: Package version. STABLE: clients may rely on this for version-skew detection.

time_sync.description: unknown for first 60s after boot; degraded if wall-clock epoch < 2023-11-15 sanity floor

errors.description: Present when status=degraded; one human-readable line per failing subsystem.

id.description: <package>:<resource> key

schema_properties.description: JSON-Schema fragment for the resource body

expires_at.description: Unix epoch seconds; null if never expires

last_used_at.description: Unix epoch seconds of last authed request; throttled to ~1/minute

rate.description: Per-token rate-limit override (req/s); null means use global

burst.description: Per-token burst override; null means use global

name.description: Same as token_id; field name varies by endpoint

scopes.description: MUST be a strict subset of the caller's own scopes; escalation returns 403 scope_escalation_blocked

bearer.description: Cleartext bearer token. Shown exactly once at mint time; uapi keeps only the salted sha256 on disk. Store securely and pass back as `Authorization: Bearer <bearer>` on subsequent requests.

name.description: Stable token id (also the uci section name). Use as the path segment in `GET /tokens/{name}`, `DELETE /tokens/{name}`. Must match `[A-Za-z0-9_]+` (uci section-name charset).

recent_errors.description: Best-effort ring of the last 20 error envelopes emitted by this uhttpd parent VM. May be empty (no errors yet, or /tmp ring file unreadable).

ts.description: Unix epoch seconds when the error was recorded.

body.description: Request body for POST/PUT/PATCH; omit for GET/DELETE

JsonPatch.description: RFC 6902 JSON Patch document. Sent with Content-Type: application/json-patch+json on PATCH. Supports ops: add, remove, replace, move, copy, test.

id.description: Optional at create: caller-supplied uci section name; becomes the response `id`. When omitted, the server emits a ULID. Read-only after create (rename via DELETE + POST). Per-resource modules may tighten further (e.g. proto=wireguard interfaces are IFNAMSIZ-tight at 15 chars).

id.description: Create-time only; picks the uci section name (which becomes the uapi `id` field). runtime.description: Populated from ubus network.interface.<name> status; empty {} when the interface has no runtime state.

client.description: Run this instance as a client (default: server).

key.description: Path to private key PEM. Write-only; reads return has_key.

bearerAuth.description: Token created via uapi-token on the router. Hashed (salted sha256) at rest.

RetryAfter.description: Seconds the client should wait before retrying (RFC 7231 6.6.4)

ETag.description: Quoted hash of the resource body (minus volatile `runtime` state). Stable across unchanged sibling sections; mutates only when the resource itself changes. Use with `If-Match` on writes (or `?if_match=` query param through uhttpd) for optimistic concurrency.

XRequestId.description: ULID echoing the request_id from request (or generated server-side).

Link.description: RFC 8288 link relations; rel="next" present when more paginated items exist.

XNextCursor.description: Convenience companion to Link rel=next: the bare next-cursor token.

IdempotentReplayed.description: Set to true when a POST response was served from the Idempotency-Key cache instead of being re-applied.

XReloadStatus.description: Outcome of the post-commit daemon reload. `ok` = init script exited 0 (NOT a runtime-convergence promise; see docs/operations.md). `no_reload` = the resource has no reload services. Absent on non-write 2xx responses.

XReloadServices.description: Comma-separated list of init scripts that were reloaded after the write committed. Absent when X-Reload-Status: no_reload.

Unauthorized.description: Missing/invalid bearer (codes: unauthorized, invalid_token; the latter also covers expired tokens and source-IP-not-permitted)

Forbidden.description: Scope or TLS check failed (codes: insufficient_scope, tls_required, scope_escalation_blocked)

PreconditionFailed.description: If-Match did not match current ETag, or JSON-Patch test op failed

ValidationFailed.description: Request body failed validation (per-field errors in `errors[]`)

Locked.description: Another write holds the same per-package lock; retry after Retry-After seconds

TooManyRequests.description: Per-token rate limit exceeded; retry after Retry-After seconds
